Title: Register a new user successfully
Preconditions: The system is up and running.
Test Steps:
1. Call the register method of the User class with valid username, password, and email.
2. Verify that the User object is created with the provided details.
3. Check that the RegistrationManager class receives the registration request.
4. Ensure the Service class updates the user's status to registered.
Expected Result: The user is registered successfully, and the system confirms the registration.

Title: Attempt to register a user with an existing username
Preconditions: A user with the username "testuser" already exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call the register method of the User class with the existing username, password, and email.
2. Verify that the User object creation fails due to duplicate username.
3. Ensure the RegistrationManager class does not process the registration request.
Expected Result: The registration fails, and an appropriate error message is returned.

Title: Access a service with a registered user
Preconditions: A user with the username "testuser" is registered.
Test Steps:
1. Call the login method of the Service class with the registered username and password.
2. Verify that the Service class authenticates the user.
3. Ensure the User class is marked as logged in.
Expected Result: The user is authenticated and granted access to the service.

Title: Attempt to access a service with invalid credentials
Preconditions: No user with the username "invaliduser" exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call the login method of the Service class with invalid username and password.
2. Verify that the Service class rejects the authentication attempt.
3. Ensure the User class remains unauthenticated.
Expected Result: The authentication fails, and an appropriate error message is returned.

Title: Process a successful registration request
Preconditions: The system is up and running.
Test Steps:
1. Call the processRegistration method of the RegistrationManager class with valid user details.
2. Verify that the RegistrationManager class creates a new User object.
3. Ensure the Service class updates the user's status to registered.
Expected Result: The registration request is processed successfully, and the user is registered.

Title: Handle a failed registration request due to duplicate username
Preconditions: A user with the username "existinguser" already exists.
Test Steps:
1. Call the processRegistration method of the RegistrationManager class with the existing username.
2. Verify that the RegistrationManager class detects the duplicate username.
3. Ensure the Service class does not update the user's status.
Expected Result: The registration request fails, and an appropriate error message is returned.

Title: Update user profile information
Preconditions: A user with the username "testuser" is registered.
Test Steps:
1. Call the updateUserProfile method of the User class with the new email address.
2. Verify that the User object's email attribute is updated.
3. Ensure the Service class reflects the updated user information.
Expected Result: The user's profile information is updated successfully.

Title: Attempt to update user profile with an invalid email format
Preconditions: A user with the username "testuser" is registered.
Test Steps:
1. Call the updateUserProfile method of the User class with an invalid email format.
2. Verify that the User object's email attribute remains unchanged.
3. Ensure the Service class does not reflect the invalid update.
Expected Result: The update fails, and an appropriate error message is returned.

Title: Log out a user
Preconditions: A user with the username "testuser" is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the logout method of the Service class with the logged-in username.
2. Verify that the User class is marked as logged out.
3. Ensure the Service class confirms the logout operation.
Expected Result: The user is logged out successfully.

Title: Attempt to log out a non-logged-in user
Preconditions: No user with the username "nonexistentuser" is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the logout method of the Service class with a non-existent username.
2. Verify that the User class does not change its logged-in status.
3. Ensure the Service class returns an appropriate error message.
Expected Result: The logout operation fails, and an appropriate error message is returned.

Title: Validate user registration request before processing
Preconditions: The system is up and running.
Test Steps:
1. Call the validateRegistrationRequest method of the RegistrationManager class with valid user details.
2. Verify that the RegistrationManager class validates the request successfully.
3. Ensure the Service class processes the validated request.
Expected Result: The registration request is validated and processed successfully.

Title: Reject a registration request with invalid email format
Preconditions: The system is up and running.
Test Steps:
1. Call the validateRegistrationRequest method of the RegistrationManager class with an invalid email format.
2. Verify that the RegistrationManager class rejects the request due to invalid email format.
3. Ensure the Service class does not process the rejected request.
Expected Result: The registration request is rejected, and an appropriate error message is returned.

3. Ensure the Service class provides the correct user information.
Expected Result: The user's information is retrieved successfully.
